headlines.title: FedEx Freight Stock Outlook After the Spin-Off and S&P 500 Debut
headlines.summary: FedEx Freight's S&P 500 debut sharpens its LTL focus, but margins, systems execution and debt will test the post-spin story.

headlines.title: FedEx (FDX) Up 1.3% Since Last Earnings Report: Can It Continue?
headlines.summary: FedEx (FDX) reported earnings 30 days ago. What's next for the stock? We take a look at earnings estimates for some clues.

headlines.title: Zacks Industry Outlook Highlights United Parcel Service, FedEx and GXO Logistics
headlines.summary: E-commerce demand, AI investments and cost controls are supporting the air freight industry, with United Parcel Service among stocks to watch.

headlines.title: FedEx unveils 2026 peak season fees, higher home delivery prices loom
headlines.summary: The carrier's holiday surcharges, which apply across various shipping services, will be higher than they were last year.

headlines.title: Is FDXF Stock a Buy With Growth Targets Amid High Debt and Macro Risks
headlines.summary: FDXF pairs scale, pricing tools and growth targets with high debt, soft demand and spin-off execution risks, making it a hold currently.

description: FedEx Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, provides transportation, e-commerce, and business services in the United States and internationally. The company operates through two segments, Express U.S. Domestic and Express International. It provides e-commerce and digital solutions; dataworks; printing and shipping management, including digital printing, professional finishing, document creation, design solutions, direct mail, signs and graphics, custom-branded boxes, copying, computer rental, Wi-Fi, corporate print solutions, shredding, U.S. passport processing and renewal, and digital notarization; packing services, as well as packing supplies and boxes; document and business services; and retail access for package transportation. In addition, the company offers logistics services, air and ocean cargo transportation, specialty transportation, customs brokerage, trade management tools and data, and door-to-door solutions; and third party logistics and supply chain management solutions, such as inbound logistics, warehousing and distribution, fulfillment, contract packaging and product configuration, systems integration, returns process and disposition, test, repair, refurbishment, and product liquidation. Further, it provides sales, marketing, administrative, information technology, and back-office support services. FedEx Corporation was founded in 1971 and is headquartered in Memphis, Tennessee.
